name: Texas Beef Bbq...on a Gas Grill
recipeInstructions:
Closely trim fat from brisket and place in basket, lightly season both sides with black pepper, garlic & onion powder, cayenne, and heavily with paprika. Place on the grill with burners on as low as possible. Place a few mesquite chips directly on the grill grate. Prop the grill hood so the front is open at least 4 inches and let cook while preparing sauce.
Texas BARBECUE SAUCE: By Susana Montes, modified by Steve Shanker
1. Melt margarine in a saucepan over low heat.
2. Add chopped onions and minced garlic; cook for about 5 minutes.
3. Stir in remaining ingredients except beer. Simmer for 15 minutes.
4. Add beer and simmer an additional 15-20 minutes, or until thickened.
5. Remove from heat and use to baste meat during grilling.
After the brisket has been grilled for approximately 30 minutes, generously coat it with barbecue sauce (adding another 10 minutes of cooking time if desired). Continue grilling, basting every 30 minutes, and adding more mesquite chips every hour until fully cooked. The sauce will form a glaze that prevents burning.
Remove meat from the grill basket and slice thinly on a bias. Serve slices with remaining barbecue sauce.
